title: Conceptual Flow from Differentiation to Partial Differentials
description: A flowchart illustrating the foundational concepts of single-variable differentiation and their extension to the multi-variable calculus required for optimization techniques.
relationships: f(x) is the foundation for understanding change
f'(x) represents the instantaneous rate of change of f(x)
f(x, y, ...) extends the concept to multiple input dimensions
Partial Derivatives measure the rate of change along one axis, holding others constant
The Gradient Vector ∇f is a vector of all partial derivatives, pointing in the direction of steepest ascent
The Directional Derivative, calculated using ∇f, gives the rate of change in any specified direction
